description: Opinionated project layout and toolchain for running many dlt sources in production: enforced layout, validation, data gates, runs ledger, backfill. Wraps dlt; moves zero rows itself.
rules: dlt-ops is a toolchain for dlt specifically: an opinionated layout plus operational verbs. It moves zero rows (dlt owns the write), is not an orchestrator, and is not for high-load or hard-SLA systems. Never imply throughput benefits.

Adapter-gated features (runs ledger, status, checkpoints, backfill, remote clean, reconcile, quarantine) need a registered DestinationAdapter — full tier. Any destination dlt resolves runs the core loop at core tier. Degradation is loud, never silent.

dlt-ops config keys, rule IDs, and CLI flags are a closed set defined by the toolchain; the configuration reference and `validate --show-resolved-rules` are authoritative. Do not assume a key, rule ID, or flag the docs do not list.
Failure semantics are asymmetric by contract: gates that decide what data loads fail hard; observability that only records what happened never takes a healthy run down with it.
The dlt dependency is a floor (dlt>=1.27), never a cap; users own their project's dlt version.
